This conference is an international forum for researchers and practitioners interested in theory and advances in intelligent systems and information technologies as well as applications of artificial intelligence methods. This year, the CISIT 2026 conference will focus on the threats and opportunities of using artificial intelligence in cybersecurity. It will be a special place for exchanging views and ideas, as well as for meetings between security specialists.

University of Siedlce
ul. 3 Maja 54
08-110 Siedlce, Poland
About Siedlce
Siedlce is a charming town in eastern Masovia, located just 100 km from Warsaw. With over 470 years of history, the city developed at the crossroads of important trade routes. Despite heavy damage during World War II, many of its historic landmarks—like the Town Hall, the Church of St. Stanislaus, and the Oginski Palace (now home to the University authorities)—have been beautifully restored.
Whether you’re an urban explorer or a nature lover, Siedlce offers plenty to enjoy:
Follow the
monument trail
, visit the
City Museum
and
Diocesan Museum
(home to Poland’s only El Greco painting)
Relax at the cinema, theater, aquapark, or enjoy local cuisine at one of the many restaurants
Discover the natural beauty of
Muchawka Lagoon
,
Alexandria Park
, and surrounding forests for walking and cycling
Getting There
90 minutes by train or car from Warsaw

Title: Submissions – CISIT 2026
Submissions – CISIT 2026
Skip to content
Explore Now
Menu
Submissions
Paper
Submission Guidelines
We invite authors to participate in CISIT 2026 by submitting
extended abstracts (5–6 pages)
as an initial step. Please follow the instructions below to complete your submission.
Submission Platform
All submissions must be made through
EasyChair
at the following link:
👉
Submit via EasyChair
If you don’t have an account, please create one here:
👉
Create EasyChair Account
If you have submitted to other conferences via EasyChair before, you may use your existing login credentials.
Call for Student Submissions
We warmly encourage students and PhD candidates to submit their work to the CISIT. Registration is free of charge for students from the University of Siedlce (UwS). A dedicated student poster session will be held during the conference, and awards will be given for the best student contributions.
Initial Submission – Extended Abstracts
Authors are invited to submit
extended abstracts (5–6 pages)
prepared using the
official
IEEE Manuscript Template
Submissions must be written in
English
, include
original and unpublished work
, and follow academic writing standards.
Figures should be submitted in
bitmap (PNG, JPG)
or, preferably,
vector format (SVG, PDF, PS)
.
All submitted abstracts will undergo
peer review
. Only positively reviewed abstracts will be accepted for presentation and publication.
Post-Conference Full Paper Submission
After the conference, authors of accepted and presented works will be invited to submit
full papers (up to 12 pages)
for
a second round of independent peer review
.
Positively reviewed papers will be considered for publication in:
📰
Studia Informatica. Systems and Information Technology
(ISSN: 1731-2264 | 20 ministerial points for Polish authors)
🔗
czasopisma.uws.edu.pl/studiainformatica
The
best contributions
will also be recommended for:
Publication in
high-impact international journals
Inclusion as
extended chapters
in the
Intelligent Systems and Information Technologies
monograph series
Each stage – abstract and full paper – is subject to a separate peer review process.
Conference Format
CISIT 2026 will be held in a
hybrid format
:
On-site participation
is highly encouraged
Remote presentations
are fully supported – authors who are unable to attend in person will have the opportunity to present online.

Skróty klawiszowe
Na stronie internetowej można korzystać ze standardowych skrótów klawiaturowych.
Informacje zwrotne i dane kontaktowe
Wszystkie problemy z dostępnością cyfrową tej strony internetowej możesz zgłosić do
Piotr Świtalski
— mejlowo
piotr.switalski@uws.edu.pl
lub telefonicznie
+48256431004
.
Każdy ma prawo wystąpić z żądaniem zapewnienia dostępności cyfrowej tej strony internetowej lub jej elementów.
Zgłaszając takie żądanie podaj:
swoje imię i nazwisko,
swoje dane kontaktowe (np. numer telefonu, e-mail),
dokładny adres strony internetowej, na której jest niedostępny cyfrowo element lub treść,
opis na czym polega problem i jaki sposób jego rozwiązania byłby dla Ciebie najwygodniejszy.
Na Twoje zgłoszenie odpowiemy najszybciej jak to możliwe, nie później niż w ciągu 7 dni od jego otrzymania.
Jeżeli ten termin będzie dla nas zbyt krótki poinformujemy Cię o tym. W tej informacji podamy nowy termin, do którego poprawimy zgłoszone przez Ciebie błędy lub przygotujemy informacje w alternatywny sposób. Ten nowy termin nie będzie dłuższy niż 2 miesiące.
Jeżeli nie będziemy w stanie zapewnić dostępności cyfrowej strony internetowej lub treści, wskazanej w Twoim żądaniu, zaproponujemy Ci dostęp do nich w alternatywny sposób.
Obsługa wniosków i skarg związanych z dostępnością
Jeżeli w odpowiedzi na Twój wniosek o zapewnienie dostępności cyfrowej, odmówimy zapewnienia żądanej przez Ciebie dostępności cyfrowej, a Ty nie zgadzasz się z tą odmową, masz prawo złożyć skargę.
Skargę masz prawo złożyć także, jeśli nie zgadzasz się na skorzystanie z alternatywnego sposobu dostępu, który zaproponowaliśmy Ci w odpowiedzi na Twój wniosek o zapewnienie dostępności cyfrowej.

About Siedlce
Siedlce is a charming town in eastern Masovia, located just 100 km from Warsaw. With over 470 years of history, the city developed at the crossroads of important trade routes. Despite heavy damage during World War II, many of its historic landmarks—like the Town Hall, the Church of St. Stanislaus, and the Oginski Palace (now home to the University authorities)—have been beautifully restored.
Whether you’re an urban explorer or a nature lover, Siedlce offers plenty to enjoy:
Follow the
monument trail
, visit the
City Museum
and
Diocesan Museum
(home to Poland’s only El Greco painting)
Relax at the cinema, theater, aquapark, or enjoy local cuisine at one of the many restaurants

Każdy ma prawo wystąpić z żądaniem zapewnienia dostępności cyfrowej tej strony internetowej lub jej elementów.
Zgłaszając takie żądanie podaj:
swoje imię i nazwisko,
swoje dane kontaktowe (np. numer telefonu, e-mail),
dokładny adres strony internetowej, na której jest niedostępny cyfrowo element lub treść,
opis na czym polega problem i jaki sposób jego rozwiązania byłby dla Ciebie najwygodniejszy.
Na Twoje zgłoszenie odpowiemy najszybciej jak to możliwe, nie później niż w ciągu 7 dni od jego otrzymania.
Jeżeli ten termin będzie dla nas zbyt krótki poinformujemy Cię o tym. W tej informacji podamy nowy termin, do którego poprawimy zgłoszone przez Ciebie błędy lub przygotujemy informacje w alternatywny sposób. Ten nowy termin nie będzie dłuższy niż 2 miesiące.
Jeżeli nie będziemy w stanie zapewnić dostępności cyfrowej strony internetowej lub treści, wskazanej w Twoim żądaniu, zaproponujemy Ci dostęp do nich w alternatywny sposób.

Jeżeli w odpowiedzi na Twój wniosek o zapewnienie dostępności cyfrowej, odmówimy zapewnienia żądanej przez Ciebie dostępności cyfrowej, a Ty nie zgadzasz się z tą odmową, masz prawo złożyć skargę.
Skargę masz prawo złożyć także, jeśli nie zgadzasz się na skorzystanie z alternatywnego sposobu dostępu, który zaproponowaliśmy Ci w odpowiedzi na Twój wniosek o zapewnienie dostępności cyfrowej.

Submissions
Paper
Submission Guidelines
We invite authors to participate in CISIT 2026 by submitting
extended abstracts (5–6 pages)
as an initial step. Please follow the instructions below to complete your submission.
Submission Platform
All submissions must be made through
EasyChair
at the following link:
👉
Submit via EasyChair
If you don’t have an account, please create one here:
👉
Create EasyChair Account
If you have submitted to other conferences via EasyChair before, you may use your existing login credentials.
Call for Student Submissions
We warmly encourage students and PhD candidates to submit their work to the CISIT. Registration is free of charge for students from the University of Siedlce (UwS). A dedicated student poster session will be held during the conference, and awards will be given for the best student contributions.
Initial Submission – Extended Abstracts
Authors are invited to submit
extended abstracts (5–6 pages)
prepared using the
official
IEEE Manuscript Template
Submissions must be written in
English
, include
original and unpublished work
, and follow academic writing standards.
Figures should be submitted in
bitmap (PNG, JPG)
or, preferably,
vector format (SVG, PDF, PS)
.
All submitted abstracts will undergo
peer review
. Only positively reviewed abstracts will be accepted for presentation and publication.
Post-Conference Full Paper Submission
After the conference, authors of accepted and presented works will be invited to submit
full papers (up to 12 pages)
for
a second round of independent peer review
.
Positively reviewed papers will be considered for publication in:
📰
Studia Informatica. Systems and Information Technology
(ISSN: 1731-2264 | 20 ministerial points for Polish authors)
🔗
czasopisma.uws.edu.pl/studiainformatica
The
best contributions
will also be recommended for:
Publication in
high-impact international journals
Inclusion as
extended chapters
in the
Intelligent Systems and Information Technologies
monograph series
Each stage – abstract and full paper – is subject to a separate peer review process.
Conference Format
CISIT 2026 will be held in a
hybrid format
:
On-site participation
is highly encouraged
Remote presentations
are fully supported – authors who are unable to attend in person will have the opportunity to present online.

About Siedlce
Siedlce is a charming town in eastern Masovia, located just 100 km from Warsaw. With over 470 years of history, the city developed at the crossroads of important trade routes. Despite heavy damage during World War II, many of its historic landmarks—like the Town Hall, the Church of St. Stanislaus, and the Oginski Palace (now home to the University authorities)—have been beautifully restored.
Whether you’re an urban explorer or a nature lover, Siedlce offers plenty to enjoy:
Follow the
monument trail
, visit the
City Museum
and
Diocesan Museum
(home to Poland’s only El Greco painting)
Relax at the cinema, theater, aquapark, or enjoy local cuisine at one of the many restaurants
Discover the natural beauty of

,

, and surrounding forests for walking and cycling

Cybersecurity in the Age of AI: Emerging Threats
and Strategic Opportunities
23-25
th
September, 2026
This conference is an international forum for researchers and practitioners interested in theory and advances in intelligent systems and information technologies as well as applications of artificial intelligence methods. This year, the CISIT 2026 conference will focus on the threats and opportunities of using artificial intelligence in cybersecurity. It will be a special place for exchanging views and ideas, as well as for meetings between security specialists.

Submissions
Paper
Submission Guidelines
We invite authors to participate in CISIT 2026 by submitting
extended abstracts (5–6 pages)
as an initial step. Please follow the instructions below to complete your submission.
Submission Platform
All submissions must be made through
EasyChair
at the following link:
👉
Submit via EasyChair
If you don’t have an account, please create one here:
👉
Create EasyChair Account
If you have submitted to other conferences via EasyChair before, you may use your existing login credentials.
Call for Student Submissions
We warmly encourage students and PhD candidates to submit their work to the CISIT. Registration is free of charge for students from the University of Siedlce (UwS). A dedicated student poster session will be held during the conference, and awards will be given for the best student contributions.
Initial Submission – Extended Abstracts
Authors are invited to submit
extended abstracts (5–6 pages)
prepared using the
official
IEEE Manuscript Template
Submissions must be written in
English
, include
original and unpublished work
, and follow academic writing standards.
Figures should be submitted in
bitmap (PNG, JPG)
or, preferably,
vector format (SVG, PDF, PS)
.
All submitted abstracts will undergo
peer review
. Only positively reviewed abstracts will be accepted for presentation and publication.
Post-Conference Full Paper Submission
After the conference, authors of accepted and presented works will be invited to submit
full papers (up to 12 pages)
for
a second round of independent peer review
.
Positively reviewed papers will be considered for publication in:
📰
Studia Informatica. Systems and Information Technology
(ISSN: 1731-2264 | 20 ministerial points for Polish authors)
🔗
czasopisma.uws.edu.pl/studiainformatica
The
best contributions
will also be recommended for:
Publication in
high-impact international journals
Inclusion as
extended chapters
in the
Intelligent Systems and Information Technologies
monograph series
Each stage – abstract and full paper – is subject to a separate peer review process.
Conference Format
CISIT 2026 will be held in a
hybrid format
:
On-site participation
is highly encouraged
Remote presentations
are fully supported – authors who are unable to attend in person will have the opportunity to present online.

About Siedlce
Siedlce is a charming town in eastern Masovia, located just 100 km from Warsaw. With over 470 years of history, the city developed at the crossroads of important trade routes. Despite heavy damage during World War II, many of its historic landmarks—like the Town Hall, the Church of St. Stanislaus, and the Oginski Palace (now home to the University authorities)—have been beautifully restored.
Whether you’re an urban explorer or a nature lover, Siedlce offers plenty to enjoy:
Follow the
monument trail
, visit the
City Museum
and
Diocesan Museum
(home to Poland’s only El Greco painting)
Relax at the cinema, theater, aquapark, or enjoy local cuisine at one of the many restaurants
Discover the natural beauty of
Muchawka Lagoon
,
Alexandria Park
, and surrounding forests for walking and cycling
Getting There
90 minutes by train or car from Warsaw

About Siedlce
Siedlce is a charming town in eastern Masovia, located just 100 km from Warsaw. With over 470 years of history, the city developed at the crossroads of important trade routes. Despite heavy damage during World War II, many of its historic landmarks—like the Town Hall, the Church of St. Stanislaus, and the Oginski Palace (now home to the University authorities)—have been beautifully restored.
Whether you’re an urban explorer or a nature lover, Siedlce offers plenty to enjoy:
Follow the
monument trail
, visit the
City Museum
and
Diocesan Museum
(home to Poland’s only El Greco painting)
Relax at the cinema, theater, aquapark, or enjoy local cuisine at one of the many restaurants
Discover the natural beauty of
Muchawka Lagoon
,
Alexandria Park
, and surrounding forests for walking and cycling
Getting There
90 minutes by train or car from Warsaw

About Siedlce
Siedlce is a charming town in eastern Masovia, located just 100 km from Warsaw. With over 470 years of history, the city developed at the crossroads of important trade routes. Despite heavy damage during World War II, many of its historic landmarks—like the Town Hall, the Church of St. Stanislaus, and the Oginski Palace (now home to the University authorities)—have been beautifully restored.
Whether you’re an urban explorer or a nature lover, Siedlce offers plenty to enjoy:
Follow the
monument trail
, visit the
City Museum
and
Diocesan Museum
(home to Poland’s only El Greco painting)
Relax at the cinema, theater, aquapark, or enjoy local cuisine at one of the many restaurants
Discover the natural beauty of
Muchawka Lagoon
,
Alexandria Park
, and surrounding forests for walking and cycling
Getting There
90 minutes by train or car from Warsaw

We invite authors to participate in CISIT 2026 by submitting

Create EasyChair Account

Call for Student Submissions
We warmly encourage students and PhD candidates to submit their work to the CISIT. Registration is free of charge for students from the University of Siedlce (UwS). A dedicated student poster session will be held during the conference, and awards will be given for the best student contributions.
Initial Submission – Extended Abstracts
Authors are invited to submit
extended abstracts (5–6 pages)
prepared using the
official
IEEE Manuscript Template
Submissions must be written in
English
, include
original and unpublished work
, and follow academic writing standards.
Figures should be submitted in
bitmap (PNG, JPG)
or, preferably,
vector format (SVG, PDF, PS)
.
All submitted abstracts will undergo
peer review
. Only positively reviewed abstracts will be accepted for presentation and publication.
Post-Conference Full Paper Submission
After the conference, authors of accepted and presented works will be invited to submit
full papers (up to 12 pages)
for
a second round of independent peer review
.
Positively reviewed papers will be considered for publication in:

(ISSN: 1731-2264 | 20 ministerial points for Polish authors)

czasopisma.uws.edu.pl/studiainformatica

best contributions
will also be recommended for:

Inclusion as
extended chapters
in the
Intelligent Systems and Information Technologies
monograph series

:

About Siedlce
Siedlce is a charming town in eastern Masovia, located just 100 km from Warsaw. With over 470 years of history, the city developed at the crossroads of important trade routes. Despite heavy damage during World War II, many of its historic landmarks—like the Town Hall, the Church of St. Stanislaus, and the Oginski Palace (now home to the University authorities)—have been beautifully restored.
Whether you’re an urban explorer or a nature lover, Siedlce offers plenty to enjoy:
Follow the
monument trail
, visit the
City Museum
and

Cybersecurity in the Age of AI: Emerging Threats

23-25
th

This conference is an international forum for researchers and practitioners interested in theory and advances in intelligent systems and information technologies as well as applications of artificial intelligence methods. This year, the CISIT 2026 conference will focus on the threats and opportunities of using artificial intelligence in cybersecurity. It will be a special place for exchanging views and ideas, as well as for meetings between security specialists.

Submissions
Paper
Submission Guidelines
We invite authors to participate in CISIT 2026 by submitting
extended abstracts (5–6 pages)
as an initial step. Please follow the instructions below to complete your submission.
Submission Platform
All submissions must be made through
EasyChair
at the following link:
👉
Submit via EasyChair
If you don’t have an account, please create one here:
👉
Create EasyChair Account
If you have submitted to other conferences via EasyChair before, you may use your existing login credentials.
Call for Student Submissions
We warmly encourage students and PhD candidates to submit their work to the CISIT. Registration is free of charge for students from the University of Siedlce (UwS). A dedicated student poster session will be held during the conference, and awards will be given for the best student contributions.
Initial Submission – Extended Abstracts
Authors are invited to submit
extended abstracts (5–6 pages)
prepared using the
official
IEEE Manuscript Template
Submissions must be written in
English
, include
original and unpublished work
, and follow academic writing standards.
Figures should be submitted in
bitmap (PNG, JPG)
or, preferably,
vector format (SVG, PDF, PS)
.
All submitted abstracts will undergo
peer review
. Only positively reviewed abstracts will be accepted for presentation and publication.
Post-Conference Full Paper Submission
After the conference, authors of accepted and presented works will be invited to submit
full papers (up to 12 pages)
for
a second round of independent peer review
.
Positively reviewed papers will be considered for publication in:
📰
Studia Informatica. Systems and Information Technology
(ISSN: 1731-2264 | 20 ministerial points for Polish authors)
🔗
czasopisma.uws.edu.pl/studiainformatica
The
best contributions
will also be recommended for:
Publication in
high-impact international journals
Inclusion as
extended chapters
in the
Intelligent Systems and Information Technologies
monograph series
Each stage – abstract and full paper – is subject to a separate peer review process.
Conference Format
CISIT 2026 will be held in a
hybrid format
:
On-site participation
is highly encouraged
Remote presentations
are fully supported – authors who are unable to attend in person will have the opportunity to present online.

About Siedlce
Siedlce is a charming town in eastern Masovia, located just 100 km from Warsaw. With over 470 years of history, the city developed at the crossroads of important trade routes. Despite heavy damage during World War II, many of its historic landmarks—like the Town Hall, the Church of St. Stanislaus, and the Oginski Palace (now home to the University authorities)—have been beautifully restored.
Whether you’re an urban explorer or a nature lover, Siedlce offers plenty to enjoy:
Follow the
monument trail
, visit the

(home to Poland’s only El Greco painting)
